explanation: This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good.  The property uses the atomic proposition "good" to represent the quality of sensor data. When "good" is false, it signifies that the sensor data is not good. The property uses the atomic propositions "state_is_NOMINAL" and "state_is_MANEUVER" to track the autopilot's state. The property ensures that whenever the system is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good, the system eventually transitions to the MANEUVER state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good.  The property uses the atomic propositions to represent the states of the autopilot and the quality of the sensor data. The property states that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good, then eventually the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state.  It does not specify the exact timing of the transition, only that it will eventually occur.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good.  It uses the atomic propositions state_is_NOMINAL, state_is_MANEUVER, and !good to represent the states and sensor data condition. The G operator ensures that this property holds globally, meaning it should always be true. The implication (->) checks if the antecedent (state_is_NOMINAL & !good) is true, and if it is, the consequent (F state_is_MANEUVER) must also be true. This means that eventually the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state after the sensor data is not good.  The property uses the atomic proposition "good" to represent the sensor data, where "!good" means the sensor data is not good. The property ensures that the transition from NOMINAL to MANEUVER occurs only when the sensor data is not good. It uses the atomic propositions state_is_NOMINAL and state_is_MANEUVER to explicitly track the autopilot state transitions. It addresses the natural language requirement in a precise manner and handles temporal aspects.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot must transition from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good.  The property uses the atomic proposition "good" to represent the sensor data. When "good" is false, it signifies that the sensor data is not good. The property ensures that whenever the system is in the NOMINAL state and the sensor data is not good, the system will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state. This is expressed using the LTL until operator (U). The property does not specify the exact timing of the transition, only that it must occur eventually.

This LTL property states that whenever the sensor data is not good (!good), the system will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state (F state_is_MANEUVER).  It addresses the natural language by capturing the requirement that a state change occurs from NOMINAL to MANEUVER when the sensor data is bad. It does not specify that it must change from NOMINAL, only that it will eventually be in MANEUVER when !good is true.

This LTL specification captures the essence of the natural language statement by expressing the condition under which a state transition from NOMINAL to MANEUVER is expected.  The formula G(!good -> F(state_is_MANEUVER)) asserts that globally, whenever !good (sensor data is not good) holds, eventually the system will transition to the MANEUVER state. It does not explicitly state that the transition is *from* NOMINAL, but if the system is already in NOMINAL, this implies that it will transition to MANEUVER.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot must transition from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good.  The property uses the atomic propositions state_is_NOMINAL, state_is_MANEUVER, and !good to represent the states and the sensor data condition. The property ensures that whenever the sensor data is not good (!good), eventually the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state. The use of the until operator (U) ensures that the transition to MANEUVER state happens while the condition of bad sensor data holds. It does not address what happens if the sensor data returns to being good or if the autopilot stays in MANEUVER state longer than necessary.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the NOMINAL state to the MANEUVER state when the sensor data is not good.  The formula first checks that the autopilot is currently in the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL). Then, it asserts that eventually (!good) will be true which means the sensor data is not good.  It then ensures that after that it is in MANEUVER state using implies and until operator (->). The use of the until operator ensures that the transition happens when sensor data is not good. We use implies operator to assert that the state transition must happen when the condition is met but there is no constraint if the sensor data is good.
